title: League One club cancels post-match press conference as manager sack looms
text: Wigan Athletic manager Ryan Lowe faces an uncertain future after the club cancelled his post-match press conference following a devastating 6-1 defeat to Peterborough United. Lowe was appointed at Wigan in March 2025 but has failed to reverse the former Premier League club's fortunes. The team currently sits 22nd in League One, two points from safety with 17 matches remaining. According to Wigan Observer reporter Paul Kendrick, 'there will be no post-match press conference' after the Peterborough match. Such cancellations typically signal an imminent managerial change. The 6-1 hammering represents a significant blow to Lowe's tenure. The club has struggled under his leadership, adopting a defensive approach focused on avoiding defeat rather than the attacking football Lowe is known for implementing. Supporters have grown frustrated with the side's lack of identity, criticizing long balls and hopeful crosses instead of entertaining, front-footed play. This cautious approach to survival football has failed to lift Wigan from relegation danger. Lowe's arrival was expected to bring positive change, but the appointment has ultimately disappointed all parties involved. With the cancellation of standard media obligations, his time at Wigan appears to be entering its final stages.
